issues.title: [BUG]The request body format for the ZhiPuAiEmbeddingModel.call method is incorrect
issues.body:
when invoke ZhiPuAiEmbeddingModel.call method, I get some error,like this  
![image](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/assets/35208417/cee15bd9-0da6-4a00-a421-9264bca2e648)  

then I check ZhiPuAiEmbeddingModel class and ZhiPuAi website  
![image](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/assets/35208417/4d432713-090d-4b13-9765-303999f62219)
![image](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/assets/35208417/c6a0decf-32d1-4ca4-9a28-eab3bc9dec4a)

in ZhiPuAiEmbeddingModel class use a Collection, but in website input is String  

Should convert the org.springframework.ai.embedding.EmbeddingRequest class to a generic type to adapt to this situation?

issues.title: Redis Vector Search is returning only 10 results
issues.body:
Hi,

I am testing Sprint AI with Redis Vector store to house my documents. However, when I perform Similarity query, I am getting only maximum of 10 results from redis.

Do I need to set any paramater to get more results?

appreciate your help

thanks

Jay

comments.body: When creating `SearchRequest`, you can use `withTopK` to specify the maximum number of results.
closing, please reopen if this did not solve your problem

issues.title: Streaming chat client does not seem to work out of the box for Azure OpenAI, but, does for Bedrock Anthropic3

comments.body: According to [the documentation](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/clients/openai-chat.html#low-level-api) the low-level API offers support for configuring streaming but, I suppose that something needs to be adapted to make it such that the detection of the call being from a streaming client needs no custom code or changes anywhere when simply calling it from the (StreamingModel) interface, that, obviously selects the correct underlying implementation?
I’ll see if I can clone the repo, run it locally somehow and if I can reproduce and subsequently fix the issue, I’ll open a PR! Thanks for the amazing work and project so far btw, super appreciated

The Azure method seems to stop the intermediate stream state as the chunks would arrive only if a tool call is happening and also skips the first element of the stream, these will be the details I’ll try to look into first!
Anyone up for reviewing this change? Thanks in advance 👌
This seems fixed in: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/178a607cf6fc65e302b7420fe50cb8dff7e2df2d closing this one!!

> This seems fixed in: [178a607](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/178a607cf6fc65e302b7420fe50cb8dff7e2df2d) closing this one!!

@bruno-oliveira Excuse me.I have tried in version 1.0.0-M1. It does not seem to be completely fixed, the problem still exists with the azureOpenai method, when the output is longer, or when calling the function.

I’ll have a try with M1 and see if it’s fixed! If not I'll reopen this
